title: Boat Rental in Agadir: Costs, Seasons and How to Choose
html:
Choosing the right boat to rent in Agadir depends on your group size, budget and preferred activity; a two-hour fishing trip from Marina d'Agadir costs around 600 MAD, while a half-day motorboat charter for six people starts at 2,500 MAD. Use BoatHire24 to compare verified boat listings and hire a boat with ease.

Where to set off in Agadir

Most charter boats depart from Marina d'Agadir, the city's main leisure port with full facilities and restaurants. Other popular departure points include the fishing port of Agadir (Port de Pêche), the sheltered bay of Anza to the north, and the quieter Tamraght beach used by smaller rib boats. Taghazout, a few kilometres north, offers direct access to surf spots and nearby islands such as La Poissonnerie.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Agadir?

Prices vary by boat type and season, with a half-day shared tour from 400 MAD per person and a full-day private catamaran from 6,000 MAD. Larger crewed yachts like the Azimut 58 can exceed 12,000 MAD for a full day, including skipper, fuel and insurance. Booking through BoatHire24 often includes transparent pricing and no hidden extras.

  • Shared fishing or sightseeing tour (2, 3 hours): from 400 MAD per person
  • Small motorboat or RIB half-day (up to 6 guests): from 2,500 MAD
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): from 6,000 MAD
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (up to 8 guests): from 10,000 MAD

Which boat to rent

  1. RIB / speedboat, best for coastal exploration and water sports, up to 8 guests, from 2,500 MAD half-day
  2. Motor yacht, spacious for cruising with amenities, up to 10 guests, from 5,000 MAD half-day; see this motor yacht as an example
  3. Catamaran, stable and family-friendly, up to 12 guests, from 6,000 MAD full-day
  4. Traditional fishing boat, authentic experience for up to 6 guests, from 800 MAD half-day

When is the best season to rent a boat in Agadir?

The best time is from April to October, when the sea is calm and temperatures range from 22°C to 28°C. The Chergui wind rarely affects the coast, making conditions ideal for sailing. Winter months (November to March) see larger swells and occasional rain, but prices can be lower.

For a calm experience, book a morning departure in May or September when the breeze is light and anchorages like Taghazout Bay are peaceful.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Agadir?

In Morocco, a licence is required for boats over 6 hp, but most rental operators include a skipper, so you do not need one yourself. The operator confirms exact requirements when you book, and for bareboat charters you may need an ICC or equivalent. Always ask BoatHire24 before confirming your trip.

At a glance

  • Price band: 400, 12,000 MAD per day
  • Peak months: April to October
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: Moroccan Dirham (MAD)

Questions about boat rental in Agadir

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Agadir?

Some operators offer bareboat rentals, but you must hold a valid licence and present it at booking; the operator will confirm the exact requirements.

What is the best boat for a family with children in Agadir?

A catamaran is the safest choice due to its stability, ample deck space, and shallow draft, allowing close approach to beaches near Anza.

How far in advance should I book a boat in Agadir?

For peak months (April, October) book at least two weeks ahead; last-minute bookings are possible in winter but availability is limited.

Are meals and drinks included in the charter price?

Most full-day charters include a basic lunch and soft drinks, but luxury yachts often include a full catering service; check the listing for details.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Agadir or list a boat for free. Whether you are a private owner or a charter company, the platform connects you with vetted renters across the globe.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Agadir and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
